originalTitle: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
summary: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer […]
content: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney’s, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he’d roll into the company’s offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the Financial Times, Eggers told the staff: > “The effect of ChatGPT on educators’ lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you’ve made every teacher’s life infinitely more difficult than it was two years ago. So, just let that settle in… If students are using it to compose, which is the biggest tragedy of all, they’ll never learn to write. And their voice is stolen from them. They’ll never have the ability to say their truth and tell their own story. And that’s silencing an entire generation or two.” To be fair, Altman likely knew what he was getting himself into. Eggers’ best-selling novel The Circle is a scathing critique of the tech industry. And he’s called AI-generated writing “pastiche nonsense.”
chineseTitle: Dave Eggers 告诉 OpenAI 员工，ChatGPT 正在“压制整整一代人的声音”
chineseSummary: 去年，Sam Altman 邀请作家 Dave Eggers 为大约 200 名 OpenAI 员工发表演讲。他写了无数的小说、剧本和新闻文章，创办了麦克斯威尼学校，并创办了多所学校和非营利组织，以更广泛地支持作家和艺术。因此，人们可能会期望他会走进公司的办公室并提供[...]
chineseContent: 去年，Sam Altman 邀请作家 Dave Eggers 为大约 200 名 OpenAI 员工发表演讲。他写了无数小说、剧本和新闻文章，创办了麦克斯威尼学校，并创办了多所学校和非营利组织，以更广泛地支持作家和艺术。因此，人们可能会期望他会走进公司的办公室，并提供如何不断多产或如何在多个领域脱颖而出的建议。相反，他显然融入了公司。据英国《金融时报》报道，艾格斯告诉工作人员：“ChatGPT 对教育工作者的生活的影响是灾难性的。无论你是否有意这样做，你都让每个老师的生活比两年前更加困难。所以，就让这个问题解决吧……如果学生用它来作曲，这是最大的悲剧，他们永远不会学会写作。他们的声音被偷走了。他们永远没有能力说出自己的真相并说出自己的想法。”这让一两代人都沉默了。”公平地说，奥特曼很可能知道自己将陷入什么境地。埃格斯的畅销小说《圆圈》对科技行业进行了严厉的批评。他称人工智能生成的写作是“模仿的废话”。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
